title: Moderate Prenatal Cadmium Exposure and Adverse Birth Outcomes: a Role for Sex-Specific Differences?
ehr_evidence: The study used data from the Avon Longitudinal Study of Parents and Children (ALSPAC), which is a population-based cohort study, not an EHR.
summary: The study investigated the association between moderate prenatal cadmium (Cd) exposure and birth outcomes (birthweight, head circumference, and crown-heel length) in a large cohort of pregnant women, specifically examining whether effects are sex-specific.
key_findings: Maternal blood Cd was adversely associated with birthweight and crown-heel length in the whole group.
In girls, maternal blood Cd was adversely associated with birthweight, head circumference, and crown-heel length, but these associations were not evident in boys.
population: Pregnant women in the Avon Longitudinal Study of Parents and Children (ALSPAC)
limitations: Sex-specific effects may mask associations in mixed-sex studies
